task_completion.description: Verify the top-level 'Digital Archives' page is created and published.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress 'Pages' list shows a published page titled 'Digital Archives' (without a 'Draft' label).

task_completion.description: Verify the top-level 'Research Access Policy' page is created and published.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress 'Pages' list shows a published page titled 'Research Access Policy' (without a 'Draft' label).

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Public Collections' page is created and published as a public page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress 'Pages' list shows a published page titled 'Public Collections' with no password protection labels.

task_completion.description: Verify the three restricted pages are created and set to password protected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WordPress 'Pages' list, the pages 'Restricted Manuscripts', 'Oral History Recordings', and 'Photographic Archives' are present and explicitly labeled as 'Password protected' next to their titles.

task_completion.description: Verify the four specific pages are set as children of 'Digital Archives'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WordPress 'Pages' list, 'Public Collections', 'Restricted Manuscripts', 'Oral History Recordings', and 'Photographic Archives' appear directly below 'Digital Archives' and their titles are prefixed with a dash (e.g., '— Public Collections'), indicating they are child pages.

task_completion.description: Verify that the created pages contain at least 100 characters of descriptive content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the WordPress block editor during the trajectory show the agent entering a substantial paragraph of text (at least ~20 words/100 characters) into the body of the pages, rather than leaving them blank or with just a few words.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the WordPress web interface to create pages.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the WordPress admin dashboard (wp-admin) and using the page editor in a web browser, rather than executing terminal commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the WordPress visibility settings to password-protect pages.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking 'Visibility: Public', selecting 'Password Protected', and entering the password in the sidebar settings, rather than just typing the password into the page's text content.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just write the expected page structure into a local text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the actual WordPress site in a browser, not a text editor like nano, vim, or gedit.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires exactly 6 pages. Top-level pages: 'Digital Archives' and 'Research Access Policy'. Child pages of 'Digital Archives': 'Public Collections', 'Restricted Manuscripts', 'Oral History Recordings', and 'Photographic Archives'. Three pages must be password protected with specific passwords: 'Restricted Manuscripts' (manuscripts2024), 'Oral History Recordings' (oralhistory2024), and 'Photographic Archives' (photos2024). All 6 pages must have at least 100 characters of content.
